log_entries.thought_preview: I have the initial overview. The sample is a Visual Basic executable with a ZIP packer (F-PROT). It drops files in Temp and Windows directories, creates persistence via Registry Run keys, and executes
The IOC summary confirms no network activity (no IPs, domains, or HTTP), but shows 3 persistence registry keys, 2 dropped files, and 12 processes created. The highlighted API call 'SetWindowsHookExA'

The dropped files are 'avscan.exe' (dropped to Temp) and 'hosts.exe' (dropped to Windows directory). The names are deceptive ('avscan' sounds like antivirus, 'hosts.exe' is a system file name). I need

The registry persistence keys confirm the malware uses the 'Run' key to execute 'avscan.exe' from the Temp folder on startup. It also modifies 'Policies\Explorer\Run' with a suspicious value 'TFGX-VD'
The process activity confirms the malware uses WScript.exe to run a VBS script (W_X_C.vbs) and cmd.exe to run a batch file (W_X_C.bat). It also deletes SafeBoot entries (a common evasion technique to
